verses.commentary: New creation day. Still dark. Mary first. Stone removed. Empty tomb discovered.
Peter and beloved disciple. Assumption: body stolen. Grave robbery feared. Resurrection not imagined.
Both go. Investigation begins.
John faster. Arrives first. Youthful speed.
Linen visible. Hesitation. Does not enter. Deference or fear.
Enters immediately. Bold. Observes grave clothes.
Soudarion (σουδάριον), face cloth. Order preserved. Not hastily unwrapped. Body passed through.
Sees and believes. First believer in resurrection. Grave clothes convince. Empty tomb plus orderly burial clothes equal resurrection.
Resurrection prophesied but not grasped. Sight precedes scriptural comprehension.
Processing. No announcement yet. Private discovery.
Mary remains. Grief. Weeping. Looks inside. Persistent seeking.
Two angels. White garments. Positioned where body lay. Head and foot. Ark of covenant imagery (Exodus 25:18-22).
They asked her, Woman, why are you crying? Who is it you are looking for? Angels inquire. Reason for tears. Object of search. Gentle questioning.
Sees Jesus. Does not recognize. Tears blur. Unexpected appearance. Resurrection body different.
Mistaken for gardener. Ironic. New Adam in garden. Requests body location. Devoted love.
Name spoken. Immediate recognition. Voice reveals identity. Rabboni: my teacher. Intimate title. John 10:3, sheep know shepherd's voice.
Relationship changing. Commission: go tell. Brothers: new status for disciples. My Father and your Father. Shared sonship. My God and your God. Shared worship.
Apostle to apostles. I have seen. Heōraka (ἑώρακα). Perfect tense. Permanent sight. Message delivered.
Locked doors. Fear. Jesus appears. Passes through barriers. Peace greeting. Shalom.
Nail prints. Spear wound. Proof of identity. Joy replaces fear. Sight produces joy.
Commission given. As Father sent Son, Son sends disciples. Apostolic mission parallels incarnation.
Genesis 2:7 echo. New creation. Receive Holy Spirit. Anticipation of Pentecost or immediate empowerment. Spirit imparted.
Apostolic power. Declarative authority. Binding and loosing. Gospel proclamation brings forgiveness or retention based on belief.
Missed first appearance. Unfortunate timing.
Thomas demands proof. Empirical requirement. Touch, not just sight. Honest doubt.
Thomas present. Doors locked again. Jesus appears. Same greeting.
Omniscience. Knew Thomas' words. Invites touch. Command to believe.
Ho kyrios mou kai ho theos mou (ὁ κύριός μου καὶ ὁ θεός μου). Lord and God. Full deity affirmed. No rebuke from Jesus. Acceptance equals validation.
But greater blessing for non-seeing faith. Future believers in view. Faith without sight is higher faith.
Many signs. Not all recorded. Limited scope acknowledged.
Why John wrote. Evangelistic intent. Believe Jesus is Messiah. Believe Jesus is Son of God. Result: life in His name. Pisteuō (πιστεύω), believe, trust. Eternal life through faith.
